description: Indian telecom giant Bharti Airtel is raising its stake in British and African ventures as it hunts for growth overseas

title: China’s National Team Seen Reducing ETF Stakes 90% in First Half
description: China’s so-called National Team will probably cut its holdings of exchange-traded funds that track domestic equities by about 90% by the end of the first half, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.

title: Traders Bet on RBA Pause With Rise in Curve-Steepening Trades
description: Australia’s weak employment report has sparked an increase in curve-steepening trades as investors bet the central bank is nearing the end of its interest-rate hiking cycle, according to traders.

title: Lionel Messi Joins Football's Billionaire Club After US Deals
description: Lionel Messi has joined Cristiano Ronaldo in football's billionaire club — and he did it without a big money Saudi contract. Rodrigo Orihuela breaks down how he got there. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: 1,000% Stock Boom Frees Vietnam Tycoon to Pour Cash Into EVs
description: Vietnam’s richest person spent billions of dollars of his personal fortune on his emerging electric automaker and other ventures last year as the shares of his corporate group soared.

title: Indonesia to ‘Listen to Market’ on New Commodity Export Body
description: Indonesia is “listening very closely to the market” as it builds a body to oversee key commodity exports, according to Pandu Sjahrir, chief investment officer of sovereign wealth fund Danantara, after a shock announcement that sowed confusion among traders and producers this week.

title: Lenovo Shares Near All-Time High After Strong AI Growth
description: Lenovo Group Ltd. shares were on track to close at their highest on Friday after the company reported strong growth in AI-related earnings that offset difficulties from rising component prices.

title: Metals Advance as Traders Track Progress in US-Iran Peace Talks
description: Copper advanced with other base metals as optimism the US and Iran can reach a peace agreement supported risk assets.

title: DeepSeek Founder Declares AGI Goal as $10 Billion Round Advances
description: DeepSeek’s senior management has told potential investors in its ongoing 70 billion yuan ($10 billion) funding round that the startup will prioritize groundbreaking AI research over short-term commercialization, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Nifty’s Technical Setup Improves While Macro Risks Persist
description: Traders are positioning for upside ahead of month-end expiry in Nifty contracts.

title: Tuas’ $1.1 Billion M1 Deal Collapses Amid Singapore Regulator Probe
description: Tuas Ltd.’s planned $1.1 billion acquisition of Singapore major telecom operator M1 Ltd. has collapsed days after regulators halted their review of the transaction.

title: Renault Joins Foreign Firm Yen Bond Offerings With Samurai Sale
description: Renault SA priced its second-largest Samurai bond offering ever, as heightened volatility in government bond markets fueled investor appetite for relatively higher-yielding but stable assets from companies like the French automaker.

title: Philippine Peso at 63.50 Per Dollar Might Be Okay, Governor Says
description: The Philippine peso at 63.50 against dollar might be okay as long as the decline is measured and not inflationary, Governor Eli Remolona said.

title: South Korea to Trim Long-Term Bond Sales as Part of June Cut
description: South Korea will mainly cut issuance of longer-dated tenor notes for its planned reduction in June bond sales, according to a top finance ministry official.

title: Philippines to Sell Up to 30B Pesos Treasury Bond Due July 2030
description: The Philippines Bureau of the Treasury in Manila said it will sell up to 30 billion pesos ($487.4 million) treasury bonds on May 26.

title: FountainVest Is Said to Mull Sale of Swiss Micro-Tools Maker SPT
description: FountainVest is considering selling Small Precision Tools, a Swiss manufacturer of high-precision components and tools, according to people familiar with the situation.

title: Polymarket Is Said to Seek Japan Market Approval in Global Push
description: Polymarket has appointed a representative in Japan and is preparing to lobby for the authorization of prediction markets in the country, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: BofA Sees Foreign Exodus from Indian Stocks Extending Into 2027
description: Foreign selling in Indian equities may stretch into next year as Asia’s artificial-intelligence winners offer stronger earnings prospects at cheaper valuations, according to BofA Global Research.

description: Honda has ordered a recall affecting up to 59,887 cars across two models, according to the National Highway Transportation Safety Administration (NHTSA).

description: Cuba's foreign minister accused Rubio of trying to "instigate a military aggression".

description: The announcement comes a week after the Pentagon cancelled a planned deployment of 4,000 troops to the country.

description: It comes just a day after the firm revealed plans for a record-breaking stock market debut.

title: ‘Cash is king’: Hong Kong, mainland Chinese firms hoard reserves as growth options narrow
description: More companies and conglomerates in Hong Kong and mainland China are hoarding cash on their balance sheets – following the approach of billionaire investor Warren Buffett’s investment vehicle – as returns from mature industries remain steady but modest amid a fragile global economic recovery. The trend has heightened investor focus on whether idle funds will be redeployed into new economy ventures, dividends or buy-backs. Cash holdings among Hong Kong- and Shanghai-listed companies most...

title: Oil Prices Rise as Traders Grow Skeptical of U.S.-Iran Deal
description: Oil prices climbed in early Asian trade on Friday as skepticism grew that U.S.-Iran negotiations would produce a breakthrough, with all the major sticking points still unresolved despite claims of progress. At the time of writing, Brent futures were trading at $104.80, 2.13% higher on the day, while WTI futures climbed to $97.99, up 1.70%. The price spike comes after both benchmarks fell roughly 2% on Thursday to their lowest levels in nearly two weeks, as traders briefly priced in the possibility of diplomatic progress. It seems that sentiment…

title: Why Saudi Arabia Is Losing Asia’s Oil Buyers
description: Saudi crude exports have been falling since the US- Iran war began, but the latest slide is no longer just a story of disrupted trade routes. Cargoes scheduled to sail from Saudi Arabia in May are now assessed at roughly 3.9 million b/d (historic lows), while almost every major Saudi buyer – China, Japan, South Korea, India and Taiwan – is cutting nominations for the months ahead. China, still Saudi Aramco’s largest customer, is expected to take only about 600,000 b/d of Saudi crude in June, roughly half April’s volume.…

title: Philip R. Lane: Europe and the world economy
Waymo expands pause to four cities as robotaxis keep driving into floods
description: Waymo's robotaxi service is now suspended in both Atlanta and San Antonio, as the company works to stop its vehicles from driving into flooded roads.

title: SpaceX scrubs first Starship V3 launch just before liftoff
description: SpaceX fueled the third-generation rocket booster and ship and was just moments away from liftoff, before calling a scrub. It's expected to try again Friday.
